entry.title: Washington Post: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Cloudburst kills at least 16 on pilgrimage in India’s Kashmir region
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Blinken criticizes China’s ‘alignment’ with Russia; divided G-20 ends
  • Sri Lankan prime minister says he’ll resign as protesters storm president’s home
  • Who could replace Boris Johnson as British prime minister?
  • Outage in Canada leaves millions without internet; ATMs, 911 lines hit
  • Japan probes Abe assassination motive as police chief admits ‘problems’
  • Abe assassination resurfaces Japan’s complex legacy in China, South Korea
  • Japan reels after assassination of Shinzo Abe, as investigation into gunman, security begins
  • Quiz: Boris Johnson did WHAT?
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: U.S. urges end to grain export blockade as Russian shelling continues
  • What are Japan’s gun laws? Abe killing shocks nation with few shootings.
  • Amazon deforestation hits six-year high in Brazil
  • Assassinations that stunned the world
  • Domestic terror cases increasingly cross borders, FBI director says
  • U.S. sending Ukraine precision artillery rounds
  • The life of former Japanese prime minister Shinzo Abe, in photos
  • Shinzo Abe, long-serving Japanese leader, slain at 67
  • Boris Johnson’s zombie government could still have some life in it
  • Ex-president of Mexico Luis Echeverria dies at 100
  • Techno party with Love Parade founder back on Berlin streets
  • Italy: All 11 hikers killed in glacier avalanche identified
  • Thousands rescued at flood-hit Hindu pilgrimage in Kashmir
  • Pamplona says no one gored after all on 3rd day of bull run
  • Millions of Muslims commemorate Eid al-Adha amid high prices
  • Srebrenica women honored for highlighting 1995 massacre
  • Therapy dogs and questions: How Highland Park’s children are coping
  • Japanese Americans shocked by Abe killing: There ‘is no safe place’
  • Without abortion, advocates worry that abuse victims will be trapped
  • A scourge of horror hits Highland Park, leaving behind death and fear
  • 10 anti-LGBTQ laws just went into effect. They all target schools.
  • Alleged Oath Keeper accused of bringing explosives to D.C. on Jan. 6
  • U.S. appeals court upholds release of Trump financial records to House
  • Trump considers waiving claim of executive privilege for Steve Bannon
  • Post Politics Now: Supreme Court ‘will not protect the rights of women,' Biden says
  • The housing market, at last, appears to be cooling off
  • Calculate how much more mortgages will cost as interest rates rise
  • As Musk moves to abandon deal, Twitter faces ‘worst case scenario’
  • Abe’s Greatest Economic Policy Legacy Is Looking Careworn
  • It Should Be Easier to Get Rid of Presidents, Too
  • Do Cities Control Their Economic Destiny?
  • The Secret Tricks To Beating Phone Addiction Shouldn’t Be Secret
  • Why Banks Face Billions in ‘Hung Debt’ as Deals Cool
  • How UK’s Tories Will Elect Leader to Replace Johnson
  • Japan’s penguins are stricken by the curse of taste
  • In Belarus, a social media post brings 6.5 years in prison
  • How the Highland Park gunman slipped through the cracks
  • U.S.-supplied artillery is helping Ukraine’s defense. Send more now.
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 9 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-09 14:39PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Significant improvement’ in young people’s willingness to join Hong Kong police, chief says, with growing interest at recruitment events
  • Hong Kong civil servant, 36, arrested over suspicious white powder sent to High Court worker
  • Hong Kong doesn’t need teachers who are against national education: John Lee
  • Hong Kong proposes 2-device cap on car dashboards, with HK$2,000 fine for offenders in safety push
  • How ‘mayor of Hong Kong’, Hilton Cheong-Leen, led push to give common people more say in British colonial era
  • ‘Where’s the common sense?’ Hong Kong justice secretary condemns claims by British lawyer trio of email threats purportedly from national security police over Jimmy Lai case
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong expert suggests end to hotel quarantine for arrivals if they take daily PCR tests and results can be provided in under 8 hours
  • Coronavirus: should Hong Kong be concerned about global rise in BA.4 and BA.5 cases? Here’s what we know about more transmissive Omicron subvariants
  • Hong Kong-mainland China scientific exchanges, research in limbo as pandemic travel restrictions drag on
  • UN Human Rights Committee grills Hong Kong officials on police handling of 2019 protests, appointment of national security law judges
  • China presents US with further demands to improve relations at meeting between Foreign Minister Wang Yi and Secretary of State Antony Blinken
  • South China Sea: US-led Southeast Asian strategy to contain Beijing would ‘complicate’ disputes, Chinese experts warn
  • Chinese nationalists celebrate Shinzo Abe shooting online, as some argue feeling happy is the normal reaction
  • China firms warn EU foreign subsidy rule could hit investor confidence
  • Four injured in knife attack at Shanghai hospital
  • China, EU spar over way forward, as human rights, Russia and trade dominate farewell event for Brussels envoy
  • Australian and Chinese foreign ministers meet as ‘first step’ in efforts to restore relations
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping conveys ‘deep regret’ over sudden death of ex-Japan leader Shinzo Abe
  • China inflation: rise in demand due to eased Covid-19 curbs forecast to drive up consumer prices
  • New US Space Force spy agency points to earthly rivalries being played out in orbit
  • On top of censorship, Chinese writers now have to contend with attacks from patriotic trolls, and that’s worrying
  • US-China trade war: Competing interests tug at Joe Biden as he mulls cutting tariffs on Chinese goods
  • Beijing grapples with Shinzo Abe’s mixed legacy on China-Japan relations after former prime minister is shot dead
  • South Korean firms fret over China supply-chain disruptions - with ‘chaos’ to continue
  • Shinzo Abe assassination: From Singapore to India, tributes flow for ardent champion of ‘free and open Indo-Pacific’
  • Sri Lanka PM to resign after protesters storm President Rajapaksa’s house
  • Indonesia could issue a fatwa that medical marijuana is halal – thanks to a mother’s love
  • ‘I feel responsible’ for Shinzo Abe shooting, police chief says as Japan struggles to come to terms with ex-PM’s death
  • Shinzo Abe’s shooter Tetsuya Yamagami initially planned to attack leader of Japan religious group that bankrupted his mum: police
  • ‘Inflation jihad’, cash handouts: Can Singapore, Malaysia and other Asian countries soften the blow of rising prices?
  • Upholding common law, tackling vested interests: Xi’s vision of Hong Kong strikes a chord
  • Lack of global consensus bodes ill for health of world’s oceans
  • How Shinzo Abe’s killer thwarted Japan’s strict gun laws
  • Date-rape drugs shock at German chancellor’s summer party
  • Russia’s aim to ‘raise true hell’ with deadly shelling overnight, war in spotlight at G20 meeting
  • Britons may hate Boris Johnson but he is still popular in Ukraine
  • WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange ‘will die’ if extradited to US, Australian journalist John Pilger says
  • The Boris Johnson era is ending. What about Westminster sleaze culture?
  • Ukraine says no power, water in Russian-held Sievierodonetsk, bodies rotting in flats
  • Elon Musk says he’s terminating US$44 billion Twitter deal
  • Daughter of Angola ex-president Jose Eduardo dos Santos demands autopsy on foul play fears
  • Russia and other nuclear-armed parties must be held to account for violations of non-proliferation treaty
  • Joe Biden signs executive order on abortion protection, calls US Supreme Court ‘out of control’
  • Prince Harry wins first stage in libel suit against UK newspaper
  • China’s largest carmaker sees NEV deliveries almost double in June as supply chain woes ease
  • Breaking up HSBC: Ping An’s push to extract value from bank’s split fails to excite shareholders as unorthodox campaign enters its third month
  • The Nothing Phone 1 is Carl Pei’s first move in trying to shake up the smartphone since leaving OnePlus. Will it work?
  • Why US talk of ‘friend-shoring’ supply chains makes no economic sense
  • Casanova: world’s greatest lover or debauched sexual predator? Harvard professor puts legendary libertine’s conquests in context
  • The fashion of South Korea’s first lady Kim Keon-hee makes her unusual – she’s unlike any of the women that came before her
  • Jung Jin-young, Cho Yi-hyun set for Summer Vacation; Sung Joon joins Tell Me It’s Love – K-drama casting latest
  • Why close a popular restaurant? A chef-owner in Hong Kong says it’s sad but to grow you must move on
  • From Sin City to Lion City, nicknames for world cities and how they were coined
  • What are China’s ‘sponge cities’? The urban flooding solution that’s being trialed across the country to counter an increasingly deadly problem
  • Why good dental hygiene is essential to reduce inflammation and slow the onset of chronic diseases and ‘inflammaging’
  • Joel Kim Booster’s recent forays into movie producing and Netflix specials – and why his job ‘isn’t to represent all of you’
  • Meet Extraordinary Attorney Woo’s Park Eun-bin, who appeared in a Taeyeon MV and alongside Lee Byung-hun and Squid Game’s Lee Jung-jae as a child star aged just six
  • Meet Ricky Martin’s adorable teen twins, Matteo and Valentino: the 50-year-old singer is raising his kids with hubby Jwan Yosef, enjoying the beach, jets and Grammys red carpets
  • Celebs who got swindled: Shark Tank star Barbara Corcoran almost lost US$400,000, Anne Hathaway got scammed out of US$2.4 million by her ex-boyfriend, and Robert De Niro got conned investing in art
  • Meet Shivon Zilis, Elon Musk’s baby mama who had twins: the tech whizz met the world’s richest man and Tesla CEO through her AI work – and fangirls over him on Twitter
  • The fabulous life of Penelope Disick: as Kourtney Kardashian and Scott Disick’s daughter turns 10, it’s clear the jet-setting, red-haired Yeezy and Skims wearing tween is a chip off the old block
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • VOX POPULI: Political assassinations not just in realm of history books
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
